observations.recommendation: The Inventory Committee include immovable properties in preparing the RPCPPE and ensure that same is prepared and signed in accordance with the requirement prescribed in Volume II of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

The Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD) Office provide sufficient evidence/supporting documents to establish that the intended recipients per payroll and DSWD form are indeed the actual recipients of the said financial assistance and the amount released to beneficiaries equates to the number of family members affected.
Ensure henceforth, that the financial statements together with other relevant documents were submitted within the reglementary period.

The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer prepare the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of DRRM Sourced from GAA and ensure its timely submission on the 5th day following the end of each month to the concerned offices.

Continually look for suitable site for a sanitary landfill that will be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of the municipality in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act; and

After finalizing the planned adaptation of the Alternative Technology, expedite its implementation to avoid congestion of unprocessed residual wastes in the dumping site.
The Agriculturist look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer include in the MEEDO budget preparation an allocation for the repayment of the subsidies received from GF;
The Municipal Accountant analyze the accounts and recognize impairment of receivables where recovery has ceased to be probable, to ensure reasonable presentation of account balances in the financial statements; and
Request authority from COA to write-off from the books, those accounts which qualify for derecognition pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008.

Maintain individual SLs for all fund transfers composing the controlling accounts “Due to NGAs” and “Due to LGUs”; and

Trace/review the Memorandum of Agreement of each fund transfers and/or coordinate with the SA to determine the proper disposition of the same and its balances, if any. Otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
The Municipal Treasurer maintain cashbook for Cash Local Treasury with DBP Acct No.  0750-009654-031.

Consider providing additional personnel to the MEO with engineering capabilities so as to increase its absorption capacity in the implementation of planned developmental projects of the municipality for timely delivery of benefits and services to constituents.

The BAC secure copies of Mayor’/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number in its procurement thru shopping prior recommendation to HOPE to award the contract; and 

The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete documentation by requiring the submission of Mayor’/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number of participating suppliers in case the municipality resorted to Shopping as its mode of procurement.

Create M & E Team to conduct a monitoring and evaluation of outcomes of LGU GAD related policies and PPAs, and prepare the required evaluation report as prescribed in Annex “H” of the JMC no. 2013-01.
Require the concerned municipal employees to update their respective accounts based on their latest salary rates to stop the accumulation of unremitted balances of GSIS Contributions.

The BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that the observers required under Section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 are invited and strictly observe the posting requirement of the Invitation to Bid, Notice of Awards, Notice to Proceed, and Approved Contracts to the PhilGEPS and LGU’s website for its widest dissemination and for the full transparency of the agency’s procurement activities.

The Municipal Accountant transfer the inappropriate charges of ₱10,000.00 from general fund to LDRRMF and stop the charging from LDRRMF of expenses that are administrative in nature and are not directly related to disaster risk reduction and management activities, otherwise, it shall be disallowed in audit.
The members of the MCPC prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, LDPC, LCC and Children’s Profile in compliance with Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
Ensure henceforth, that the financial statements together with other relevant documents were submitted within the reglementary period.

The Municipal Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the agency’s receivable accounts and determine the debtors and/or implementing government entities concerned in case of fund transfers and to maintain and update individual subsidiary ledgers composing the accounts; and

If the subject receivable accounts were non-moving for ten (10) years or more, and settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, request for its write-off following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2005.

Continually look for suitable site for a sanitary landfill that will be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of the municipality in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act; and

After finalizing the planned adaptation of the Alternative Technology, expedite its implementation to avoid congestion of unprocessed residual wastes in the dumping site.
The members of the MCPC prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, LDPC, LCC and Children’s Profile in compliance with Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
The Agriculturist look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.

The Inspection and Appraisal Committee facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Supply Officer expedite the preparation of the property cards of all PPEs as per available records of the accounting office to reduce the unreconciled difference of ₱274.128 million;
The Inventory Committee include immovable properties in preparing the RPCPPE and ensure that same is prepared and signed in accordance with the requirement prescribed in Volume II of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Maintain individual SLs for all fund transfers composing the controlling accounts “Due to NGAs” and “Due to LGUs”; and
Trace/review the Memorandum of Agreement of each fund transfers and/or coordinate with the SA to determine the proper disposition of the same and its balances, if any. Otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before 15th day after the end of each month;
The Municipal Accountant maintain separate SLs for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the STF by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor; and
Ensure henceforth, that recording of transfer of Unspent DRRMF is based on validated RAAOs for accurate presentation of balances in the financial statement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Supply Officer trace back all inventories already issued based on the Requisition and Issue Slip and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued, and make the appropriate adjusting entries to properly account those already issued in the books of the Municipality;
The Municipal Accountant henceforth, prepare the corresponding JEV on issuance of inventories based on the SSMI submitted by the Supply Officer to avoid accumulation of inventory accounts; and
Henceforth, require the Appraisal and Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventory items in the custody of the Supply Officer to determine undistributed/unconsumed items, if any, and prepare a corresponding report thereof.
The Supply Officer, in coordination with the Accounting Office, look for any available records/documents that will aid in the preparation of PCs of all the PPEs of the Municipality.
The Municipal Accountant comply with Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs in accounting for inventories.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer  regularly monitor all the programs and projects of the municipality and submit a monitoring report as basis to issue memorandum to the implementing office to ensure full implementation of the development projects; and

The MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer identify in the AIP and SAAOB, respectively, the specific names and locations of developmental projects under Barangay Energization/Water System and Construction/Rehabilitation of Drainage appropriation to better evaluate its eligibility under 20% DF.

The BAC and BAC Secretariat ensure that the observers required under Section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 are invited and strictly observe the posting requirement of the Invitation to Bid, Notice of Awards, Notice to Proceed, and Approved Contracts to the PhilGEPS and LGU’s website for its widest dissemination and for the full transparency of the agency’s procurement activities;

The Municipal Engineer ensure that pictures before, during and after construction are taken for each infrastructure projects which should be properly labelled as to name of the project, location, and status of construction. Embedded items should also be given emphasis in the captured pictures; and

Beneficiaries (farmers’ association/
cooperative) of farm machineries be required to register and be accredited with the office of the Sangguniang Bayan of Makato to ensure that these are bonafide peoples’ organizations or civil society organizations and or cooperative.

We recommended that the Inspection and Appraisal Committee facilitates the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The General Services Office and the Accounting Office conduct reconciliation of their PPE records to ascertain their differences and make the necessary adjustments in order to establish the completeness, accuracy and existence of the PPE accounts presented in the Financial Statements; and
The General Services Office and Inventory Committee include immovable properties in preparing the PC and RCPPPE, respectively.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant always consider the nature and purpose of fund transfers received by the municipality and any issuances related thereto for its proper accounting; and
The Municipal Accountant transfer the remaining balance of the TL-DRRMF account from GF to TF and make the appropriate adjusting entries to properly account the same in the respective books.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer revert the unutilized balances of the appropriations classified as MOOE to the general fund so that funds thereof may be used as Supplemental Budget and can be appropriated for other projects of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Development Coordinator ensure that balances of appropriations for MOOE are reverted to the general fund at the end of the fiscal year and such shall not be made continuing in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

The Inventory Committee ensure that the RPCPPE were prepared in accordance with the prescribed format, by type of PPE and with complete data/information for easy reconciliation;

The Accounting Office religiously update the PPELC and ensure that balances thereof tally with the balances presented in the trial balance and/or financial statement; and

The General Services Office and the Accounting Office expedite the reconciliation of their PPE records to ascertain their differences and make the necessary adjustments in order to establish the completeness, accuracy and existence of the PPE accounts presented in the Financial Statements.

Reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office. Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can be made easier.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

a.	The OIC-Municipal Accountant maintain individual subsidiary ledgers for all fund transfers composing the controlling accounts “Due to NGAs” and “Due to LGUs;”

b.	The OIC-Municipal Accountant identify/trace the difference of ₱2.332 million and ₱107,986.33 in the “Due to NGAs” and “Due to LGUs”, account, respectively, per source agency/per project under Trust Fund and maintain subsidiary ledgers thereto; and

c.	The OIC-Municipal Accountant trace up the Memorandum of Agreement covering the fund transfers with completed PPAs but with fund balances as indicated in Appendix C and refund the remaining balance to the source agency, if required.

We recommended and the management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary correcting entries to properly account the unutilized balance of BGCM fund amounting to ₱171,682.00 and maintain separate subsidiary ledger thereof in compliance with LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 and pertinent provisions of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant immediately reconcile their records/books to determine the necessary corrections from the unreconciled difference of ₱66,035.42; and


Henceforth, conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be timely adjusted and posted in their respective books.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:

The Budget Officer properly classify the 70 percent allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response as to its expense classification such as MOOE or CO in preparing the Annual Budget and Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations;

The LDRRMO, Budget and Accounting Office reconcile and determine from the P15.331 million the total unexpended/unobligated balance of LDRRMF for CO that should be made continuing in the General Fund books and the correct balance of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE that should be transferred to the Trust Fund books; and 

The OIC-Municipal Accountant transfer the correct unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE for CY 2020 and prior years in the Trust Fund books under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM”. Likewise, subsidiary ledgers shall be maintained for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Municipal Officials to exert concerted efforts to intensify collections of long outstanding RPT and SET Receivables including the interest thereon so that more development programs may be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to: 

Direct the concerned official to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) and/or Local Government Units (LGUs) to the Municipality of Makato for proper evaluation of the fund utilizations. 

Submit the lacking Certificate of Funds Availability of the related transactions composing the ₱132,332.33 disbursements from Trust Fund.

Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements from trust funds are supported with corresponding MOA/Trust Agreement.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Ensure that the required reports are prepared by the proper officials concerned, in accordance with the prescribed format, and submitted within the reglementary period; and 

Remind the concerned officials to be mindful of their corresponding roles in the administration of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in order to enhance the agency’s capacity to strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system, through decentralized powers, responsibilities and resources.

We recommended the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the agency’s receivable accounts and determine the debtors and/or implementing government entities concerned in case of fund transfers and to maintain and update individual subsidiary ledgers composing the accounts.

If the subject receivable accounts were non-moving for ten (10) years or more, and settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, request for its write-off following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2005.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the prior years’ unremitted Due to GSIS and make representations with the GSIS, if needed, for any reconciling items.

We recommended the following:
	
In reorganizing the MCPC, update its mandated duties and responsibilities in conformity with Rule 18 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9344, as amended, to fully uphold the essence of the institutionalization of LCPC and to clearly sets out the duties and responsibilities of the council in strengthening the juvenile justice and welfare of children at risk and children in conflict with the law from prevention to rehabilitation and reintegration; and

The management to direct the members of the MCPC to prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, LDPC, LCC and Children’s Profile in compliance with Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Social Welfare and Development Office and Local Planning and Development Office to develop their 3-year Comprehensive Local Juvenile Intervention Program in accordance with the guidelines for the LGU on the Development of the CLJIP, electronic copy of which can be downloaded from the JJWC National Secretariat website, pursuant to DILG MC No. 2016-68.

We recommended that all the beneficiaries (farmers’ association/
cooperative) of farm machineries be required to register and be accredited with the office of the Sangguniang Bayan of Makato to ensure that these are bonafide peoples’ organizations or civil society organizations and or cooperative.

We recommended that:

Require the Agriculturist to submit report on the result of the inspection/ investigation conducted, to the Local Chief Executive, copy furnish the office of the auditor.

Look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.

We recommended that the Inspection and Appraisal Committee facilitates the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended the following:
For the inventory committee to regularly conduct the annual physical count of property, plant and equipment and prepare the corresponding report thereon; and
Thereafter, reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office.  Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can be made easier.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the concerned official to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) and/or Local Government Units (LGUs) to the Municipality of Makato for proper evaluation of the fund utilizations.
Submit the lacking Certificate of Funds Availability of the related transactions composing the ₱132,332.33 disbursements from Trust Fund.
Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements from trust funds are supported with corresponding MOA/Trust Agreement.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
The Budget Officer to properly classify the 70 percent allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness as to its functional classification such as MOOE or CO/Infrastructure in the preparation of Annual Budget;
Reconciliation should be made between the LDRRMO, Budget and Accounting Office to trace up and determine the MOOE & Capital Outlay component of the prior years’ unexpended balances of LDRRMF in order to facilitate the transfer of appropriate funds to the Special Trust Fund; and
Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant should transfer to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” in the Trust Fund books the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2018 and prior years. Subsidiary ledgers shall be maintained for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Ensure that the required reports are prepared by the proper officials concerned, in accordance with the prescribed format, and submitted within the reglementary period; and
Remind the concerned officials to be mindful of their corresponding roles in the administration of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in order to enhance the agency’s capacity to strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system, through decentralized powers, responsibilities and resources.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare annually the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM projects, activities or program using the prescribed format as attached herein, taking into consideration the Annual Plan and Budget for LDRRM and the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements from LDRRMF are duly supported with LDRRMFIP.

We recommended the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the agency’s receivable accounts and determine the debtors and/or implementing government entities concerned in case of fund transfers and to maintain and update individual subsidiary ledgers composing the accounts.
b.	If the subject receivable accounts were non-moving for ten (10) years or more, and settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, request for its write-off following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2005.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer to update the PPE Property Cards, conduct the physical count of PPE and prepare the corresponding Report thereon. After which, the reconciliation of records between the Accounting and General Services be made in order to establish the accuracy and validity of the agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment account.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the contributions and loan amortizations withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges. 

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the prior years’ unremitted Due to GSIS and make representations with the GSIS, if needed, for any reconciling items.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Council for the Protection of Children be reorganized to conform to the membership requirement under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 in order to optimize the involvement of other mandated members; 	
	
In reorganizing the MCPC, update its mandated duties and responsibilities in conformity with Rule 8 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9344, as amended, to fully uphold the essence of the institutionalization of LCPC and to clearly sets out the duties and responsibilities of the council in strengthening the juvenile justice and welfare of children at risk and children in conflict with the law from prevention to rehabilitation and reintegration; and

The management to direct the members of the MCPC to prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, LDPC, LCC and Children’s Profile in compliance with Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Social Welfare and Development Office and Local Planning and Development Office to develop their 3-year Comprehensive Local Juvenile Intervention Program in accordance with the guidelines for the LGU on the Development of the CLJIP, electronic copy of which can be downloaded from the JJWC National Secretariat website, pursuant to DILG MC No. 2016-68.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC Chairman to use the Philippine Bidding Documents prepared by the Government of the Philippines (GOP) and strictly observe the procedures and practices presented therein which are for mandatory use in projects that are financed in whole or in part by the agency. Attached herewith is the prescribed format of Invitation to Bid for ready reference, marked as Annex H. Moreover, please submit the printed copy of Invitation to Bid posted in the PHILGEPS after the end of the posting period and duly indicated with page number.

Nevertheless, for detailed guidelines, the Philippine Bidding Documents for Procurement of Infrastructures as well as for Goods and Consultancy Services are downloadable from GPPB Website

We recommended that Municipal Mayor through his Department Heads to:

Hasten the implementation of the programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries; and

Require or assign Official to closely monitor the fund releases coming from the National Government to ensure the speedy implementation of the projects.

We recommended that all the beneficiaries (farmers’ association/cooperative) of farm machineries be required to register and be accredited with the office of the Sangguniang Bayan of Makato to ensure that these are bonafide peoples’ organizations or civil society organizations and or cooperative.

We recommended that:

Require the Agriculturist to submit report on the result of the inspection/ investigation conducted, to the Local Chief Executive, copy furnish the office of the auditor.

Look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.

To ensure that the same deficiency be avoided, we recommend the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank (AGDB), within a day or within a week if collection reaches P5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality.  To ensure that this is being done, require her to submit for notation by your good office all copies of validated deposits slips daily or regularly.

We recommended that the Inspection and Appraisal Committee facilitates the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended the following:
a.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the agency’s receivable accounts and determine the debtors and/or implementing government entities concerned in case of fund transfers and to maintain and update individual subsidiary ledgers composing the accounts.
b.	If the subject receivable accounts were non-moving for ten (10) years or more, and settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, request for its write-off following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2005.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer to update the PPE Property Cards, conduct the physical count of PPE and prepare the corresponding Report thereon. After which, the reconciliation of records between the Accounting and General Services be made in order to establish the accuracy and validity of the agency’s Property, Plant and Equipment account.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the contributions and loan amortizations withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the prior years’ unremitted Due to GSIS and make representations with the GSIS, if needed, for any reconciling items.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Council for the Protection of Children be reorganized to conform to the membership requirement under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 in order to optimize the involvement of other mandated members;
In reorganizing the MCPC, update its mandated duties and responsibilities in conformity with Rule 8 of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9344, as amended, to fully uphold the essence of the institutionalization of LCPC and to clearly sets out the duties and responsibilities of the council in strengthening the juvenile justice and welfare of children at risk and children in conflict with the law from prevention to rehabilitation and reintegration; and
The management to direct the members of the MCPC to prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, LDPC, LCC and Children’s Profile in compliance with Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Local Social Welfare and Development Office and Local Planning and Development Office to develop their 3-year Comprehensive Local Juvenile Intervention Program in accordance with the guidelines for the LGU on the Development of the CLJIP, electronic copy of which can be downloaded from the JJWC National Secretariat website, pursuant to DILG MC No. 2016-68.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor through the BAC Chairman to use the Philippine Bidding Documents prepared by the Government of the Philippines (GOP) and strictly observe the procedures and practices presented therein which are for mandatory use in projects that are financed in whole or in part by the agency. Attached herewith is the prescribed format of Invitation to Bid for ready reference, marked as Annex H. Moreover, please submit the printed copy of Invitation to Bid posted in the PHILGEPS after the end of the posting period and duly indicated with page number.
Nevertheless, for detailed guidelines, the Philippine Bidding Documents for Procurement of Infrastructures as well as for Goods and Consultancy Services are downloadable from GPPB Website.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant should prepare the PC, PPELC and RPCPPE in accordance with Section 119, 120 and 124 of MNGAS for NGAs, Volume I and adhere with the prescribed formats under Annexes 9, 37 and 49 of the same manual, Volume II. The Municipal Accountant should also prepare the RPLC following the format and instructions under Annex 11 of the same Manual. 

Thereafter, a reconciliation of balances should be made between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Verify any noted discrepancy and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective books of accounts in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.   Likewise, in order to avoid or lessen the incurrence of stale checks, require the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month after its issuance or even shorter, if possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Maintain cashbooks for Cash In Treasury and Cash Advances by fund and shall be updated and balanced daily and accomplish the certification every end of the month; and

Transactions starting her assumption as Acting Municipal Treasurer should be recorded immediately and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor through his Department Heads to:

Hasten the implementation of the programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries; and

Require or assign Official to closely monitor the fund releases coming from the National Government to ensure the speedy implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the Management:

Cause the immediate implementation of the development projects in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint MC 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to regularly monitor all the programs and projects of the municipality and submit a monitoring report as basis for the former to issue memorandum to the implementing office to ensure full implementation of the development projects; and

Re-align funds of prior year’s continuing appropriation that have not been implemented for over three years to identified priority projects duly approved by the Municipal Development Council.

We recommended that all beneficiaries (farmers’ association/cooperative) of farm machineries be required to register and be accredited with the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan of Makato to ensure that these are bonafide peoples’ organizations or civil society organizations and or cooperative.  


Likewise, we recommended that for equitable distribution of financial or technical assistance to the marginalized sector of the Municipality, (tothe farmers’ associations and the like), the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist shall extend to at least one association per barangay with consideration on the financial capabilities of the members and the number of members of such barangay.

We recommended that the Management should implement the items not installed and/or justify why such were not accomplished. Otherwise, the amount of P201,742.58 will be disallowed through the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance. Install the one unit downspout where it should be supposed to be located.

We recommended that:

Require the Agriculturist to submit report on the result of the inspection/investigation conducted, to the Local Chief Executive, copy furnish the office of the auditor.

Look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.

To ensure that the same deficiency be avoided, we recommend the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank (AGDB), within a day or within a week if collection reaches P5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality.  To ensure that this is being done, require her to submit for notation by your good office all copies of validated deposits slips daily or regularly.

We recommended that the Management should implement the items not installed and/or justify why such were not accomplished. Otherwise, the amount of P201,742.58 will be disallowed through the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance. Install the one unit downspout where it should be supposed to be located.

Instruct the maintenance crew to ensure that the drainage must always be free of debris to serve the purpose of its installation.

We recommended that the Inspection and Appraisal Committee facilitates the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

We recommended that the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant should prepare the PC, PPELC and RPCPPE in accordance with Section 119, 120 and 124 of MNGAS for NGAs, Volume I and adhere with the prescribed formats under Annexes 9, 37 and 49 of the same manual, Volume II. The Municipal Accountant should also prepare the RPLC following the format and instructions under Annex 11 of the same Manual.
Thereafter, a reconciliation of balances should be made between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Verify any noted discrepancy and effect the necessary adjustments.
The management should also ensure the timely submission of the required reports so as to give ample time for its review and verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective books of accounts in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.   Likewise, in order to avoid or lessen the incurrence of stale checks, require the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month after its issuance or even shorter, if possible.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
Maintain cashbooks for Cash In Treasury and Cash Advances by fund and shall be updated and balanced daily and accomplish the certification every end of the month; and
Transactions starting her assumption as Acting Municipal Treasurer should be recorded immediately and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor through his Department Heads to:
Hasten the implementation of the programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries; and
Require or assign Official to closely monitor the fund releases coming from the National Government to ensure the speedy implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the Management:
Cause the immediate implementation of the development projects in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint MC 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017;
The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to regularly monitor all the programs and projects of the municipality and submit a monitoring report as basis for the former to issue memorandum to the implementing office to ensure full implementation of the development projects; and
Re-align funds of prior year’s continuing appropriation that have not been implemented for over three years to identified priority projects duly approved by the Municipal Development Council.

We recommended that all beneficiaries (farmers’ association/cooperative) of farm machineries be required to register and be accredited with the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan of Makato to ensure that these are bonafide peoples’ organizations or civil society organizations and or cooperative.
Likewise, we recommended that for equitable distribution of financial or technical assistance to the marginalized sector of the Municipality, (to the farmers’ associations and the like), the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist shall extend to at least one association per barangay with consideration on the financial capabilities of the members and the number of members of such barangay.

We recommended that:

Require the Agriculturist to submit report on the result of the inspection/investigation conducted, to the Local Chief Executive, copy furnish the office of the auditor.

Look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.

The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers for the receivable accounts in order to facilitate its confirmation and validation.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2016 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee to conduct physical count of PPE in CY 2016, and every year thereafter; and prepare a RPCPPE; and require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of records.

Further, we recommend that the municipal accountant transfer the public infrastructures - Roads and Bridges (251) amounting to P28,057,445.25 to the respective RPI, except for completed public infrastructures funded out of a loan which shall be retained in the books of accounts until the loan is fully paid. Also, monitor the Construction in Progress account and close the completed projects to the appropriate asset accounts.
observations.reason:
Reiterated as Finding No. 1

(RPCPPE was prepared but not supported with PCs and PPELCs.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit advance copies of purchase orders to the Audit Team Leader within five (5) days from their issuance.  Likewise, submit Inspection and Acceptance Reports/notifications within twenty four hours (24) of actual delivery of goods/services/projects to allow the auditor to conduct timely inspection of deliveries on a test basis.

To ensure that the same deficiency be avoided, we recommend the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank (AGDB), within a day or within a week if collection reaches P5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality.  To ensure that this is being done, require her to submit for notation by your good office all copies of validated deposits slips daily or regularly;

Require the accountable officer to submit monthly report of collections and deposits duly certified by the Municipal Accountant to be posted in the bulletin of the Municipality of Makato as a regular monitoring tool; and

After three (3) written reminders to the accountable officer, file an appropriate administrative case against the said accountable officer.
observations.reason: (There were still instances of undeposited collections)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Cause the immediate implementation of the development projects in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint MC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011;

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to regularly monitor all the programs and projects of the municipality and submit a monitoring report as basis for the former to issue memorandum to the implementing office to ensure full implementation of the development projects.

Re-align funds of prior year’s continuing appropriation that have not been implemented for over two years to identified priority projects duly approved by the Municipal Development Council.
observations.reason: (Unimplemented Projects were still noted, hence, reiterated as Finding No. 5)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads to hasten the implementation of programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries of said programs and projects.  Likewise, to require the conduct of regular monitoring of all accomplishments of the different departments versus the targeted programs and projects of the Municipality and submit the required quarterly reports to the office of the Team Leader, Team 4.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an official/s or committee to monitor regularly the programs and projects based on the calendar of activities/targets of the implementing offices.
observations.reason: (Unutilized funds/ unimplemented projects were noted, hence, reiterated as Finding No. 4)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that: 

The Local Chief Executive issue Office Order to the Municipal Agriculturist to conduct inspection/investigation in order to validate the existence of the borrowers based on the list submitted.

Submit report on the result of the inspection/investigation conducted, to the Local Chief Executive, copy furnish the office of the auditor.
Exert effort to collect the receivable.
The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers for the receivable accounts in order to facilitate its confirmation and validation.
observations.managementAction:
The management validation on the borrowers is on-going.

Reiterated as Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
The projects totaling P1,333,452.74 should be re-aligned to projects allowable under JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

For the ensuing year the Municipality plan for the 20% Development Fund projects that reflect the priorities in the Annual Investment Program and other local development plans and yet at the same time are eligible under the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.  Other projects not otherwise eligible should be funded from the regular budget of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management should implement the items not installed and/or justify why such were not accomplished. Otherwise, the amount of P201,742.58 will be disallowed through the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance. Install the one unit downspout where it should be supposed to be located.

Instruct the maintenance crew to ensure that the drainage must always be free of debris to serve the purpose of its installation.

We further recommended that they submit a program of work and accomplishment report pertinent to exact amount of P201,742.58 for the uninstalled and unaccomplished items of work, complete with details and dates of installation/accomplishment.

We recommended that:
Require the Agriculturist to submit report on the result of the inspection/investigation conducted, to the Local Chief Executive, copy furnish the office of the auditor.
Look into the whereabouts of the farmer beneficiaries and exert extra effort to collect the receivables.
The Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary ledgers for the receivable accounts in order to facilitate its confirmation and validation.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand from the AO with outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2016 the immediate liquidation of such cash advances with the provision that if not liquidated within 30 days upon receipt of the demand letter, the same shall be deducted from their salaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a committee to conduct physical count of PPE in CY 2016, and every year thereafter; and prepare a RPCPPE; and require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Inspection and Appraisal Committee facilitates the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

(a) Stop the charging of payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF;
(b)	Submit documents to prove the establishment of extension classes duly recommended by DECS Regional Director and approved by the DECS Secretary as required under JC No. 01-A

We recommended that management submit advance copies of purchase orders to the Audit Team Leader within five (5) days from their issuance.  Likewise, submit Inspection and Acceptance Reports/notifications within twenty four hours (24) of actual delivery of goods/services/projects to allow the auditor to conduct timely inspection of deliveries on a test basis.

To ensure that the same deficiency be avoided, we recommend the following:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact the daily collections with the authorized depository bank (AGDB), within a day or within a week if collection reaches P5,000.00 or more considering the distance of the AGDB from the municipality.  To ensure that this is being done, require her to submit for notation by your good office all copies of validated deposits slips daily or regularly;
Require the accountable officer to submit monthly report of collections and deposits duly certified by the Municipal Accountant to be posted in the bulletin of the Municipality of Makato as a regular monitoring tool; and
After three (3) written reminders to the accountable officer, file an appropriate administrative case against the said accountable officer.

We recommended that the Management:
Cause the immediate implementation of the development projects in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the DILG-DBM Joint MC 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011;
The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to regularly monitor all the programs and projects of the municipality and submit a monitoring report as basis for the former to issue memorandum to the implementing office to ensure full implementation of the development projects.
Re-align funds of prior year’s continuing appropriation that have not been implemented for over two years to identified priority projects duly approved by the Municipal Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads to hasten the implementation of programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries of said programs and projects.  Likewise, to require the conduct of regular monitoring of all accomplishments of the different departments versus the targeted programs and projects of the Municipality and submit the required quarterly reports to the office of the Team Leader, Team 4.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate an official/s or committee to monitor regularly the programs and projects based on the calendar of activities/targets of the implementing offices.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submits the year-end financial statements that are compliant with the New Chart of Accounts and the PPSAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the year-end financial statements on February 14, 2017 that are compliant with the New Chart of Accounts and the PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly follow the rules and regulations in submission of year end trial balances and financial statements as prescribed under Section 41 (2) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the year-end trial balances and financial statements on February 14, 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the legal basis for the exemptions, if any so that the excess expenditure in PS Cost may be passed in audit.  Also, submit justification why such were incurred despite the declaration of the SP of the approved budget to be “inoperative in part”.

We recommend that the BAC verify, validate and ascertain all statements made and documents including the NFCC submitted by the bidders to ensure compliance with the procurement laws and regulations and the timely delivery of procured goods.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers in all accounts as required under Sec. 10 of NGAs, for LGUs, Vol. II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied to the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that a committee shall be created to conduct physical count of PPE in CY 2015, and every year thereafter; and prepare a RPCPPE; and require the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of PPE, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation of records.

Further, we recommend that the municipal accountant transfer the public infrastructures - Roads and Bridges (251) amounting to P22,201,752.57 to the respective RPI, except for completed public infrastructures funded out of a loan  which shall be retained in the books of accounts until the loan is fully paid. Also, monitor the Construction in Progress account and close the completed projects to the appropriate asset accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The management has created an Inventory Committee to take the physical inventory of all PPE.

Reiterated as Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality should enact a new municipal ordinance to address the problems on Solid Waste in accordance with the mandates of RA 9003. Moreover, we recommend that the penal sanctions be included in the said municipal ordinance that will be enacted to ensure cleanliness within the Municipality and could provide additional income.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order No.2016-29 by the Municipal Mayor on Re-Constituting the Mun Solid Waste Management Board pursuant to RA 9003 and Accomplishment Report for CY 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Unit refile request for the write-off upon completion of documentary requirements and procedures. Pending the approval of the said request, the said accounts shall be transferred to the Registry of Dormant Account following the guidelines and procedures in dropping of accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 2007.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create an Inspection and Appraisal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided under Section 379 of RA 7160 and Section 125 of MNGAS Vol. 1 Annex 51 on the use of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP)
observations.managementAction:
The management has created its appraisal committee and identified its unserviceable properties and working out for its disposal.

Reiterated as Finding No. 5

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to regularly monitor all the programs and projects of the municipality and submit a monitoring report as basis for the former to issue memorandum to the implementing office to ensure full implementation of the development projects.
